title: SketchCircles Object
description: The collection of circles in a sketch. This provides access to the existing circles and supports the methods to create new circles.

methods.name: addByCenterRadius
methods.description: Creates a sketch circle that is always parallel to the x-y plane of the sketch and is centered at the specified point.

methods.name: addByThreePoints
methods.description: Creates a sketch circle that passes through the three points. The three points must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch.

methods.name: addByThreeTangents
methods.description: Creates a sketch circle that is tangent to the three input lines. The three lines must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch.

methods.name: addByTwoPoints
methods.description: Creates a sketch circle where the circle passes through the two points and the distance between the two points is the diameter of the circle.

methods.name: addByTwoTangents
methods.description: Creates a sketch circle that is tangent to the two input lines. The two lines must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch circle using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of circles in the sketch.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Create circle by center and radius API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a sketch circle by the center and radius.

related_samples.name: Create Circle By 3 Tangents API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates three lines and then draws a circle that is tangent to the lines. It then creates tangent constraints to maintain that relationship.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

related_samples.name: Simple Extrusion Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new extrusion feature, resulting in a new component.

related_samples.name: Simple Revolve Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new revolve feature, resulting in a new component.

addByCenterRadius.title: SketchCircles.addByCenterRadius Method
addByCenterRadius.description: Creates a sketch circle that is always parallel to the x-y plane of the sketch and is centered at the specified point.
addByCenterRadius.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchCircle object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: The center point of the circle. It can be an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the circle in centimeters.

related_samples.name: Create circle by center and radius API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a sketch circle by the center and radius.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

related_samples.name: Simple Extrusion Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new extrusion feature, resulting in a new component.

related_samples.name: Simple Revolve Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new revolve feature, resulting in a new component.

addByThreePoints.title: SketchCircles.addByThreePoints Method
addByThreePoints.description: Creates a sketch circle that passes through the three points. The three points must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch.
addByThreePoints.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchCircle object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first point that the circle will pass through. The z component must be zero.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The second point that the circle will pass through. The z component must be zero.

parameters.name: pointThree
parameters.description: The third point that the circle will pass through. The z component must be zero.

addByThreeTangents.title: SketchCircles.addByThreeTangents Method
addByThreeTangents.description: Creates a sketch circle that is tangent to the three input lines. The three lines must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch.
addByThreeTangents.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchCircle object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: tangentOne
parameters.description: The first line that the circle will be tangent to. The line must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch and cannot be parallel to the second or third line.

parameters.name: tangentTwo
parameters.description: The second line that the circle will be tangent to. The line must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch and cannot be parallel to the first or third line.

parameters.name: tangentThree
parameters.description: The third line that the circle will be tangent to. The line must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch and cannot be parallel to the first or second line.

parameters.name: hintPoint
parameters.description: A point that specifies which of the possible multiple solutions to use when creating the circle. If you consider the three input lines to be infinite there are many possible solutions when creating a circle that is tangent to all three lines. The hint point is a point anywhere within the area defined by the three lines where the circle is to be created.

related_samples.name: Create Circle By 3 Tangents API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates three lines and then draws a circle that is tangent to the lines. It then creates tangent constraints to maintain that relationship.

addByTwoPoints.title: SketchCircles.addByTwoPoints Method
addByTwoPoints.description: Creates a sketch circle where the circle passes through the two points and the distance between the two points is the diameter of the circle.
addByTwoPoints.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchCircle object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines a point is sketch space and lies on the x-y plane of the sketch.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines a point is sketch space and lies on the x-y plane of the sketch.

addByTwoTangents.title: SketchCircles.addByTwoTangents Method
addByTwoTangents.description: Creates a sketch circle that is tangent to the two input lines. The two lines must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch.
addByTwoTangents.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchCircle object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: tangentOne
parameters.description: The first line that the circle will be tangent to. The line must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch.

parameters.name: tangentTwo
parameters.description: The second line that the circle will be tangent to. The line must lie on the x-y plane of the sketch and cannot be parallel to the first line.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the circle in centimeters.

parameters.name: hintPoint
parameters.description: A point that specifies which of the possible four solutions to use when creating the circle. If you consider the two input lines to be infinite they create four quadrants which results in four possible solutions for the creation of the circle. The hint point is a point anywhere within the quadrant where you want the circle created.

classType.title: SketchCircles.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: SketchCircles.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch circle using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: SketchCircles.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of circles in the sketch.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchCircles_var.count

isValid.title: SketchCircles.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchCircles_var.isValid

objectType.title: SketchCircles.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

propertyValue = sketchCircles_var.objectType
